content:
- Continued bug-real-binary-tests-are-optional-via-early-return from TO BE VERIFIED, ran deep skeptical verification sweep over all real-binary skip call sites, and updated plan to include missing coverage (dcs real-etcd tests + both HA real e2e scenarios in test-real) before promoting marker to NOW EXECUTE.
- Implemented fail-fast real-binary enforcement end-to-end: removed Option/env-gated skip behavior from src/test_harness/binaries.rs, changed helper signatures to non-optional, and refactored all real-test callers (pg16, etcd3, pginfo, process worker, dcs real fixture, ha e2e resolvers/tests) to fail fast instead of returning Ok(()).
- Updated Makefile and RUST_SYSTEM_HARNESS_PLAN.md to remove PGTUSKMASTER_REQUIRE_REAL_BINARIES gating semantics, expanded test-real to include real dcs + both HA e2e scenarios, and added AGENTS learning about RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 stabilizing long real e2e gate runs.
- Verification: focused targeted suites all passed; required gates passed with evidence logs under .ralph/evidence/bug-real-binary-tests-are-optional-via-early-return (make check, make test, make test-bdd, make lint). make test had one transient unassisted HA flake on the first parallelized run; rerun with RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 passed cleanly.
- Completion: updated task file to done/passing, ran .ralph/task_switch.sh, and next immediate step is stage+commit all changed files with required task-finished commit message.

- Read progress log for Choose-Task and reviewed .ralph/current_tasks.md fully.
- Applied bug-first/meta-task rules: no bug tasks are failing, but there are non-bug tasks with <passes>false</passes>, so those take priority over meta-task 18.
- Evaluated all failing tasks 27-33, checked blockers and priorities (none explicit). Selected .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/27-task-e2e-ha-stress-workloads-during-role-changes.md as the next logical unblocked continuation after task 26 in the same HA/e2e chain.
- Wrote selected path into .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next cycle should execute task 27 and run full required validation gates.

- Picked up current task .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/27-task-e2e-ha-stress-workloads-during-role-changes.md and confirmed it was not started with no lifecycle marker.
- Ran broad parallel exploration across src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs, API contracts, harness modules, Makefile gates, and prior task-file lifecycle patterns to ground the plan in current code reality.
- Wrote a full Draft 1 execution plan into task 27 with: 12 completed exploration tracks, concrete module/function-level implementation phases, stress scenario definitions (switchover/failover/no-quorum under workload), artifact path strategy under .ralph/evidence/27-e2e-ha-stress, and 15 parallel implementation tracks.
- Appended TO BE VERIFIED marker at end of task file and intentionally did not execute code/test changes this run per requested workflow.
- Next step after restart: read task 27, detect TO BE VERIFIED, perform deep skeptical verification using 16+ parallel verification tracks, alter at least one plan item, then replace marker with NOW EXECUTE.

- New request from PO email: use token from sibling docs repo to push pgtuskmaster_rust with strict no-bashrc/no-token-leak constraints.
- Read progress log first per AGENTS instructions, then sent frequent status emails to PO (ack, plan, pre-check start, pre-check done/start push, push done, final summary).
- Located token source file safely by filename discovery and key-only extraction: ../pgtuskmaster-docs/.env.local contains DOCS_GITHUB_TOKEN.
- Pre-push verification before first push attempt: scanned working tree + outgoing commits + broad history patterns for github_pat_/ghp_/x-access-token; no matches in rust repo content/commits.
- Executed one-shot push without persistent config/profile edits: sourced env in-process, created temporary askpass script, used GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 + git -c credential.helper= push https://github.com/djosh34/pgtuskmaster_rust.git HEAD:master.
- Push succeeded (HEAD -> master), and remote HEAD verification via ls-remote matched local HEAD.
- Important self-note: one exploratory command against docs repo remote printed an embedded token in that repo’s remote URL; avoid remote -v on repos that may already contain inline PATs in future secret-safe tasks.
- Next if resumed: optionally sanitize docs repo remote URL to remove inline PAT and switch to askpass-based auth there too (only if explicitly requested).
- Sent an additional PO follow-up email flagging non-blocking security issue: docs repo origin URL appears to include inline PAT in git config (pre-existing), recommended separate cleanup task.

- Received PO mail asking to confirm next user can plain `git push` and if not, make it so.
- Followed mail-update protocol with frequent replies: ack, plan, subtask start/completion, and final summary.
- Verified current state first: branch master, origin was SSH, no local/global credential helper.
- Tested non-interactive plain push path: `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 git push --dry-run origin HEAD:master` failed with `Permission denied (publickey)`.
- Implemented repo-local persistent auth (no global config changes):
  - set origin to `https://github.com/djosh34/pgtuskmaster_rust.git`
  - set local helper `credential.helper=store --file=.git/.credentials`
  - approved github credential with username `x-access-token` and password from `../pgtuskmaster-docs/.env.local` (`DOCS_GITHUB_TOKEN`)
- Verified success with plain commands and no manual prep:
  - `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 git push origin HEAD:master` => Everything up-to-date
  - `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 git push` => Everything up-to-date
- Final state check: expected HTTPS origin, expected local credential helper, credential file present.
- Next if resumed: if security hardening requested, rotate token and migrate from plaintext credential store to safer credential helper, but current PO requirement ("just works") is satisfied.

- Received PO mail "Missing main or entry" with explicit instruction to use add-task-as-agent and set high priority; did not modify product code.
- Followed mail cadence strictly: sent immediate ack, sent plan after analysis, sent start-subtask update, sent completion/next-subtask update, and sent final summary email with created task paths.
- Used add-task-as-agent skill and created three new high-priority tasks in story-rust-system-harness:
  - 34-task-add-non-test-unified-node-entrypoint-autobootstrap-and-ha-loop.md
  - 35-task-migrate-all-node-startup-tests-to-unified-entrypoint-config-only.md (blocked_by 34)
  - 36-task-enforce-post-startup-hands-off-test-policy-no-direct-coordination.md (blocked_by 35)
- Verified files and metadata (status not_started, passes false, priority high, dependency chain present).
- Next step after quitting: if requested, refine acceptance checklists further into concrete file/module inventories before execution agents pick up tasks.

- Received PO mail "Push instructions" asking to update Ralph do-prompt so it asks to git push when task is completed.
- Followed required mail cadence: immediate ack, plan update after locating file, subtask start, subtask completion, and final summary pending.
- Located target file `.ralph/ralph-do-task.md` ("do promt md" reference) and made one focused edit in completion checklist: added `- [ ] push commits with: `git push`` directly after commit instructions.
- Verified file content with sed to ensure the new push step is present in the right section.
- Next if resumed: no further work required unless PO asks for wording tweaks or to propagate this instruction to other prompt variants.

- Received PO email requesting: add those new tasks, commit, and push; followed email cadence with frequent status replies.
- Verified tasks 34/35/36 already existed as new files with expected high-priority metadata and dependency chain (35 blocked by 34, 36 blocked by 35).
- Confirmed .ralph/current_tasks.md included entries for the new tasks.
- Created focused commit with only task artifacts: .ralph/current_tasks.md plus the three new task files.
- Commit: 79ee8e8 ("Add high-priority unified-entrypoint follow-up tasks (34-36)").
- Pushed successfully to origin/master: 45dee44..79ee8e8.
- Left unrelated pre-existing working tree changes untouched as requested scope was only adding tasks + commit + push.

- Processed PO email "Is ralph going?" and followed frequent-reply workflow.
- Verified user systemd services: ralph-pgtuskmaster.service is active/running; opencode-ralph also active.
- Confirmed recent movement in journal logs for ralph-pgtuskmaster: fresh entries at 2026-03-03T12:19:43+0100 and 12:19:45+0100, checked at 12:19:51+01:00.
- Found separate issue: ralph-event-watch.service in restart loop (exit 127) due to missing ExecStart script path under an old ElixirPGTuskMaster directory.
- Created bug file via add-bug skill: .ralph/tasks/bugs/ralph-event-watch-missing-script-path.md (no fix applied).
- Sent multiple status emails to PO: start ack, plan, subtask starts/completions, issue notice, final summary.
- Next time: if asked, investigate/fix ralph-event-watch service path/config; otherwise leave as documented bug.
